A practical validation pipeline:
(a) DWT denoising + segmented averaging: Use db4/db6, 4–5 levels; estimate noise σ from highest details, apply soft-threshold (σ√(2 log N)), reconstruct. Form a short QRS-centered segment (±40–60 ms) and, if more beats exist, average aligned segments to suppress noise/artifacts.
(b) Outlier tests: From denoised signal, derive a robust baseline using off-QRS windows (median/IQR). Compute z-scores for amplitude and first-difference (slope). Flags: |z_amp|>6, |z_slope|>6, or IQR-based outliers. Also compute QRS width at 50% of R (expected ~60–120 ms).
(c) DTW/TAD to normal template: Normalize (z-score or RMS), align to a normal QRS template with DTW; accept if DTW distance within ≤3 MAD of a reference distribution or correlation ≥0.9. TAD across multiple beats, if available, strengthens the decision.
Cross-lead consistency (if multilead): Pearson r≥0.7 within QRS with lead-appropriate polarity, lag ≤10 ms; MI >0.3 bits. Inconsistency suggests artifact.
Preliminary assessment of the provided 128 ms segment: clear sharp R (~1.73) followed by S (~−0.68), estimated R–S separation ≈26 samples (~52 ms), morphology plausible for a physiological QRS.
